top_findings.summary: The skill's A/B testing implementation requires persistent user IDs (`hash(user_id)`) to assign users to variants, which enables tracking of individual users over time. For the stated purpose of "collecting and using user feedback" to improve AI systems, aggregated and anonymized feedback data is sufficient. Persistent user identification is not necessary for analyzing feedback patterns, running improvement cycles, or conducting A/B tests (session-based or anonymous assignment methods would suffice).
top_findings.impact: OVERREACH: Data Over-collection - The skill's A/B testing implementation requires persistent user IDs (`hash(user_id)`) to assign users to variants, which enables tracking of individual users over time. For the stated purpose of "collecting and using user feedback" to improve AI systems, aggregated and anonymized feedback data is sufficient. Persistent user identification is not necessary for analyzing feedback patterns, running improvement cycles, or conducting A/B tests (session-based or anonymous assignment methods would suffice).
